rationale: Keep: the citing paper’s core efficacy–appeal trade-off (rigid plans seen as more effective but less pleasant) closely aligns with the cited paper’s findings that specific scheduling makes activities feel more work-like and less enjoyable. This makes the 2016 JMR article a plausible mechanism/theory source for why rigidity can reduce appeal, not an obviously incidental mention. The pair is DOI-matched directly (10.1509/jmr.14.0591), so it warrants full relation judgment.
